subject: Chemistry
title: Imatinib: The First Targeted Cancer Drug
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Imatinib: The First Targeted Cancer Drug.

hook.text: Imatinib turned chronic myeloid leukemia from a death sentence into a manageable disease. It was the first precision cancer drug.

buildup.text: CML is caused by one mutation: the Philadelphia chromosome creates a faulty protein (BCR-ABL) that tells cells to divide forever.

discovery.text: Imatinib was designed to fit precisely into the BCR-ABL protein's active site, blocking it like a key jammed in a lock.

twist.text: Before imatinib, CML patients survived 3-5 years. With it, survival is near-normal lifespan—one pill daily controls the disease.

climax.text: Imatinib proved that understanding a cancer's specific chemistry lets you design a precise weapon against it.

punchline.text: One mutation, one drug, one target. Precision medicine's first triumph.

quiz.question: What makes imatinib different from traditional chemotherapy?
options: It targets only the specific mutant protein causing the cancer, sparing healthy cells
It kills all rapidly dividing cells in the body
It strengthens the immune system to fight cancer naturally
